name: Nairobi

palette.name: City Canopy Green
palette.rationale: Green references Nairobi's parks, urban tree cover, and proximity to Nairobi National Park.

palette.name: Savanna Ochre
palette.rationale: An editorial earth tone for savanna grasses, dry-season light, and surrounding highland soil.

palette.name: Harambee Black
palette.rationale: Black is drawn from Kenya's national flag, a civic symbol frequently present in the capital.

palette.name: Flag Red
palette.rationale: Red is drawn from Kenya national-symbol context and is treated separately from editorial natural colors.

palette.name: Highland Haze
palette.rationale: A soft off-white editorial tint represents highland haze, bright skies, and sunlit open space around the city.

palette.name: Civic Concrete
palette.rationale: A blue-gray editorial neutral references Nairobi's civic buildings, roads, and expanding high-rise fabric.

sources.title: Nairobi City County
sources.note: Used for official city and county context.

sources.title: Kenya National Flag
sources.note: Used to distinguish the national-symbol black from editorial natural colors.

notes: Seed record; natural and architectural tones are editorial, while black and red are treated as official national-symbol context visible in the capital.
editorialSummary: Nairobi's expanded starter palette balances green canopy, ochre savanna, national-symbol black and red, highland haze, and civic concrete for a city identity shaped by nature, government, and growth.
